---
title: "Untitled"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
editor: visual
---

```{r}
#| message: false
library('tidyverse')
library(DHARMa)
set.seed(123)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Data Preprocessing

```{r}
tplant <- read_csv(
  file = "data/tplant_observations - main_2025-05-05.csv",
  na = c("Na", "n", "na"),
  col_types = cols(
    ID = col_character(),
    Tray = col_factor(),
    Row = col_integer(),
    Column = col_integer(),
    n_shoot_axis = col_integer(),
    Leaf_shape = col_factor(),
    Leaf_edge = col_factor(),
    Comments = col_character()
  )
) |>
  filter(!(is.na(ID))) |> # remove plants without ID/label
  mutate(
    # change pop 165 to 156
    ID = case_when(ID == "165-1" ~ "156-1",
                   ID == "165-2" ~ "156-2",
                   ID == "165-3" ~ "156-3",
                   TRUE ~ ID),
    Pop = stringi::stri_extract(ID, regex = "^.*(?=-)"),
    # extract Population number from the ID
    Replicate = stringi::stri_extract(ID, regex = "(?<=-).*$"),
    # extract Replicate number from the ID
    Leaf_shape = case_when(Leaf_shape == "o" ~ "O", Leaf_shape == "l" ~ "L", TRUE ~ Leaf_shape),
    Leaf_edge = case_when(Leaf_edge == "s" ~ "S", Leaf_edge == "g" ~ "G", TRUE ~ Leaf_edge)
  ) |>
  select(ID, Pop, Replicate, everything()) 
```

```{r}
ID_is_not_alive_A_tha <- c(
  "384-20",
  "486-7",
  "463-5",
  "384-12",
  "346-15",
  "384-15",
  "472-6",
  "408-5",
  "408-9",
  "346-16",
  "482-5",
  "247-1",
  "465-1",
  "409-12",
  "409-18",
  "409-19",
  "409-5",
  "409-14",
  "494-18",
  "406-15",
  "406-17",
  "438-9",
  "466-21",
  "346-14",
  "463-1",
  "345-7",
  "471-8",
  "452-5",
  "406-18",
  "452-8",
  "345-10",
  "342-13",
  "406-19",
  "406-20",
  "342-12",
  "345-20",
  "159-5",
  "459-13",
  "451-16",
  "375-1",
  "471-9",
  "475-3",
  "45-18",
  "471-5",
  "344-10",
  "471-6",
  "454-1",
  "7-5",
  "375-5",
  "487-10",
  "479-20",
  "468-1",
  "375-4",
  "442-4",
  "408-8",
  "346-8",
  "409-13",
  "375-2",
  "346-12",
  "480-10",
  "346-13",
  "409-6",
  "409-20",
  "205-1",
  "470-3",
  "34-16",
  "176-2",
  "360-1",
  "160-2",
  "345-4",
  "345-13",
  "472-13",
  "345-18",
  "213-6",
  "494-19",
  "486-16",
  "494-17",
  "347-17",
  "104-9",
  "213-4",
  "408-7",
  "205-5",
  "184-1",
  "187-2",
  "433-1",
  "475-6",
  "409-10",
  "494-16",
  "409-7",
  "180-8",
  "488-19",
  "409-9",
  "409-8",
  "409-17",
  "267-21",
  "407-14",
  "465-2",
  "468-2",
  "494-7",
  "100-1",
  "348-18",
  "7-17",
  "468-15",
  "491-4",
  "406-10",
  "493-1",
  "308-6",
  "308-8",
  "308-14",
  "308-19",
  "310-1",
  "315-3",
  "308-10",
  "308-4",
  "308-3",
  "310-19"
)

is_below_18cm <- read_csv(file = "data/F5_below_18cm.csv")

Pop_pool_sampled <- c("1", "7", "8", "24", "27", "34", "35", "41", "45", "47", "49", "102", "104", "105", "113", "122", "159", "168", "169", "172", "173", "177", "180", "183", "184", "203", "232", "233", "258", "263", "264", "265", "267", "282", "310", "342", "344", "345", "346", "347", "348", "357", "359", "384", "405", "406", "408", "428", "433", "438", "439", "447", "448", "449", "451", "453", "458", "459", "466", "468", "472", "474", "477", "478", "479", "480", "481", "482", "483", "484", "486", "487", "488", "489", "491", "494")

Pop_indv_sampled <- c("19", "24", "29", "41", "45", "94", "105", "106", "113", "120", "122", "161", "167", "177", "182", "187", "191", "205", "206", "213", "233", "241", "255", "264", "357", "359", "361", "375", "407", "409", "424", "429", "438", "441", "444", "450", "452", "454", "456", "462", "463", "469", "470", "471", "475", "476")
# Pop 409 changed from Pool to individual sampled, sampled 1, 2, 3, 4, 11; Pop 407 changed from Pool to individual

#ID_skipped_sampling <- c("172-5", "180-3", "180-4", "180-7", "180-11", "184-1", "184-5", "122-18", "122-20", "408-7", "346-16", "408-7", "120-2", "182-4", "205-1", "205-5", "24-5", "24-12", "24-18", "24-20", "1-13", "7-5", "7-8", "45-8", "45-10", "45-11", "45-12", "45-20", "159-3", "159-4", "159-9", "159-10", "159-13", "168-12", "168-14", "168-16", "177-4", "177-8", "177-9", "177-10", "177-12", "177-13", "177-14", "177-16", "177-18", "177-20", "233-7", "468-12", "408-3")

# the column sampletype shows the assigned type of sampling per population, but
# gives no information if the replicate was sampled or not
tplant <- tplant |>
  mutate(
    is_alive_A_tha = case_when(!(ID %in% ID_is_not_alive_A_tha) ~ "TRUE", TRUE ~ "FALSE"),
    sampletype = case_when(
      Pop %in% Pop_pool_sampled &
        Pop %in% Pop_indv_sampled ~ "Pool_and_Indv",
      Pop %in% Pop_pool_sampled &
        !(Pop %in% Pop_indv_sampled) ~ "Pool",
      !(Pop %in% Pop_pool_sampled) &
        Pop %in% Pop_indv_sampled ~ "Indv",
      TRUE ~ "Not_sampled"
    ),
    is_above_18cm = case_when(ID %in% is_below_18cm$F5_is_below_18cm ~ FALSE,
                              TRUE ~ TRUE)
  )

  
```

```{r}
# Summing up replicates for each population
summed_pop <- tplant |>
  filter(is_alive_A_tha == TRUE)|>
  group_by(Pop) |>
  summarise(total_repl = n()) |>
  arrange(desc(total_repl))

summed_pop
```

```{r}
# add label for threshold of total replicats above 5
summed_pop_above_3 <- summed_pop |>
  mutate(pop_above_3 = ifelse(total_repl > 3, T, F))

summed_pop_above_3
```

```{r}
summed_pop_above_3 |>
  filter(pop_above_3 == T) |>
  nrow()
```

Out of the 164 populations 112 have more than 3 replicates, 54 less.

The following Population have not 4 replicates.

```{r}
# Populations below threshold of replicates
tplant_pop_below_3 <- summed_pop_above_3 |>
  filter(pop_above_3 == F) 

tplant_pop_below_3$Pop
```

```{r}
# Add the above to the tplant df
tplant <- tplant |>
  group_by(Pop) |>
  filter(is_alive_A_tha == TRUE)|>
  mutate(total_repl_in_pop = n()) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(pop_above_3 = ifelse(total_repl_in_pop > 3, T, F),
         pop_below_15 = ifelse(total_repl_in_pop < 15, T, F)) |>
  relocate(Comments, .after = last_col())


tplant
```

```{r}
# Add the ecosystem type to the tplant df
Phenology <- read_csv(file = "data/Phenology_field_2.csv",
                               col_types = cols(
                                 Site_ID = col_character(),
                                 Type = col_factor(),
                                 First_FT_julianday = col_double()
                               )) |>
  select(Site_ID, Type)

tplant <- tplant |>
  left_join(Phenology, join_by("Pop" == "Site_ID")) |>
  mutate(
    Type = case_when(
      Type == "wall" ~ "Wall",
      Type == "grass" ~ "Grass",
      Type == "pavement" ~ "Pavement",
      TRUE ~ Type
    )
  )
```

Make leaf shape and leaf edge binary:

```{r}
tplant <- tplant |>
  mutate(elongated = case_when(Leaf_shape == "L" ~ 1,
                                Leaf_shape == "O" ~ 0,
                                T ~ NA),
         serrated = case_when(Leaf_edge == "S" ~ 1,
                               Leaf_edge == "G" ~ 0,
                               T ~ NA))
```

## F5 Analysis (Transplanted)

### EDA of Leaf Shape and Leaf Edge per Environment

```{r}
Ecotype_colors <- c(
  "wall" = "#f8cd37",
  "grass" = "#6a176e",
  "pavement" = "#bc3754",
  "Tree bed" = "#f37819"
)

# Hist pop per eco type
pop_per_eco <- tplant |>
  distinct(Pop, .keep_all = TRUE) |>
  select(Pop, Type) |>
  group_by(Type) |>
  summarise(n_pop_per_eco = n())

ggplot(data = pop_per_eco,
       mapping = aes(y = n_pop_per_eco, x = Type, fill = Type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
  "Wall" = "#f8cd37",
  "Grass" = "#6a176e",
  "Pavement" = "#bc3754",
  "Tree bed" = "#f37819"
)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman")
  ) +
  labs(y = "Number of Populations per Type")
```

```{r}
# Hist replicates per eco type
repl_per_eco <- tplant |>
  select(Replicate, Type) |>
  group_by(Type) |>
  summarise(n_repl_per_eco = n())

ggplot(data = repl_per_eco,
       mapping = aes(y = n_repl_per_eco, x = Type, fill = Type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
  "Wall" = "#f8cd37",
  "Grass" = "#6a176e",
  "Pavement" = "#bc3754",
  "Tree bed" = "#f37819"
)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman")
  ) +
  labs(y = "Total Individuals of the Populations by Type")
```

```{r}
leaf_shape_proportions <- tplant |> 
  drop_na(Leaf_shape) |>
  count(Type, Leaf_shape, name = "count") |> 
  group_by(Type) |> 
  mutate(proportion = round(count / sum(count), 2)) |> 
  select(-count) |> 
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = Leaf_shape, 
    values_from = proportion,
    names_prefix = "prop_",  
    values_fill = 0
  ) |> 
  ungroup() |>
  select(-prop_)

leaf_shape_long <- leaf_shape_proportions |> 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(prop_L, prop_O),
    names_to = "Leaf_shape",
    values_to = "Proportion"
  )

ggplot(leaf_shape_long, 
       aes(x = Type, y = Proportion, fill = Leaf_shape)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("prop_L" = "#6a176e", "prop_O" = "#f8cd37"),
    labels = c("Elongated", "Oval")
  ) +
  labs(x = "Type", y = "Proportion", fill = "Leaf Shape") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman")
  )
```

```{r}
leaf_edge_proportions <- tplant |> 
  drop_na(Leaf_edge) |>
  count(Type, Leaf_edge, name = "count") |> 
  group_by(Type) |> 
  mutate(proportion = round(count / sum(count), 2)) |> 
  select(-count) |> 
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = Leaf_edge, 
    values_from = proportion,
    names_prefix = "prop_",  
    values_fill = 0
  ) |> 
  ungroup() |>
  select(-prop_)

leaf_edge_long <- leaf_edge_proportions |> 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(prop_G, prop_S),
    names_to = "Leaf_edge",
    values_to = "Proportion"
  )

ggplot(leaf_edge_long, 
       aes(x = Type, y = Proportion, fill = Leaf_edge)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("prop_G" = "#6a176e", "prop_S" = "#f8cd37"),
    labels = c("Not Serrated", "Serrated")
  ) +
  labs(x = "Type", y = "Proportion", fill = "Leaf Edge") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman")
  )
```

```{r}
leaf_edge_shape_proportions <- tplant |> 
  drop_na(Leaf_edge, Leaf_shape) |>
  mutate(Leaf_edge_shape = paste0(Leaf_shape, Leaf_edge)) |>
  count(Type, Leaf_edge_shape, name = "count") |> 
  group_by(Type) |> 
  mutate(proportion = round(count / sum(count), 2)) |> 
  select(-count) |> 
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = Leaf_edge_shape, 
    values_from = proportion,
    names_prefix = "prop_",  
    values_fill = 0
  ) |> 
  ungroup() |>
  select(-prop_, -prop_Lx)

leaf_edge_shape_long <- leaf_edge_shape_proportions |> 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(prop_LG, prop_LS, prop_OG, prop_OS),
    names_to = "Leaf_edge_shape",
    values_to = "Proportion"
  )


ggplot(leaf_edge_shape_long, 
       aes(x = Type, y = Proportion, fill = Leaf_edge_shape)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("prop_LG" = "#440154", "prop_LS" = "#31688e",
             "prop_OG" = "#35b779", "prop_OS" = "#fde725"),
    labels = c("Long Smooth", "Long Serrated", "Oval Smooth", "Oval Serrated")
  ) +
  labs(x = "Type", y = "Proportion", fill = "Leaf Edge Shape") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman")
  )



```

```{r}
# Hist replicates per eco type
axis_per_eco <- tplant |>
  select(n_shoot_axis, Type) |>
  drop_na(Type, n_shoot_axis) |>
  group_by(Type) |>
  summarise(mean_axis = round(mean(n_shoot_axis), 1))

ggplot(data = axis_per_eco,
       mapping = aes(y = mean_axis, x = Type, fill = Type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
  "Wall" = "#f8cd37",
  "Grass" = "#6a176e",
  "Pavement" = "#bc3754",
  "Tree bed" = "#f37819"
)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman")
  ) +
  labs(y = "Average Shoot Axis per Type")
```

```{r}
# distribution of sampled pop across ecotpy?
dist_sampled_pop_per_eco <- tplant |>
  filter(pop_above_3 == T,
         sampletype != "Not_sampled") |>
  distinct(Pop, .keep_all = T) |>
  group_by(Type) |>
  summarise(n_sampled_pop = n() )

ggplot(data = dist_sampled_pop_per_eco,
       mapping = aes(y = n_sampled_pop, x = Type, fill = Type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
  "Wall" = "#f8cd37",
  "Grass" = "#6a176e",
  "Pavement" = "#bc3754",
  "Tree bed" = "#f37819"
)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman")
  ) +
  labs(y = "Populations with Leafs Sampled from")
```

### MANOVA

Is there a Population-level difference between the observed traits between the types of Environment?

Since the \~2000 plants are coming from Populations, individuals from the same Population are more similar to each other, creating non-independence. To address this a proportion of the type of leaf edge, leaf shape and height is calculated per population.

```{r}
# compute a ratio of leaf shape and leaf edge for each population from the individuals in each population
ratio_trait_per_pop <- tplant |>
  drop_na(Leaf_shape, Leaf_edge) |> # remove plants, which could not be categorized
  group_by(Pop, Type) |> # group by Population
  summarise(
    # compute the log ratio of elongated leaf shape over oval leaf shape
    n_L = sum(Leaf_shape == "L"), # sum up the count of elongated leaf per population
    n_O = sum(Leaf_shape == "O"), # sum up the count of oval leaf per population
    # avoid division by 0 by adding 0.01 (laplace smoothing)
    log_ratio_L_O = log((n_L + 0.01) / (n_O + 0.01)),
    # zero means same proportion, positive number means more elongated leaf shape (L), negative oval leaf shape (O)
    
    # compute the log ratio of serrated leaf edge over not serrated leaf edge
    n_S = sum(Leaf_edge == "S"),# sum up the count of serrated leaf per population
    n_G = sum(Leaf_edge == "G"),# sum up the count of not serrated leaf per population
    log_ratio_S_G = log((n_S + 0.01) / (n_G + 0.01)),
    
    # compute the log ratio of plant height
    is_above_18cm = sum(is_above_18cm == T),
    is_below_18cm = sum(is_above_18cm == F),
    log_ratio_plant_heigth = log((is_above_18cm + 0.01) / (is_below_18cm + 0.01))
  ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(Type = as.factor(Type)) 
```

#### MANOVA Assumptions

```{r}
table(ratio_trait_per_pop$Type)

colnames(ratio_trait_per_pop)

boxplot(log_ratio_L_O ~ Type, data = ratio_trait_per_pop) # wall has outliers
boxplot(log_ratio_S_G ~ Type, data = ratio_trait_per_pop)
boxplot(log_ratio_plant_heigth ~ Type, data = ratio_trait_per_pop) # pavement and tree bed outlier

library(mvnormtest)

by(ratio_trait_per_pop[,c("log_ratio_L_O","log_ratio_S_G", "log_ratio_plant_heigth")], ratio_trait_per_pop$Type, 
   function(x) mshapiro.test(t(x)))
# violation of normality 

library(biotools)
boxM(ratio_trait_per_pop[,c("log_ratio_L_O","log_ratio_S_G", "log_ratio_plant_heigth")], ratio_trait_per_pop$Type)


pairs(ratio_trait_per_pop[,c("log_ratio_L_O","log_ratio_S_G", "log_ratio_plant_heigth")])

cor(ratio_trait_per_pop[,c("log_ratio_L_O","log_ratio_S_G", "log_ratio_plant_heigth")])

```

MANOVA assumptions not met (normality violation). Using PERMANOVA instead.

#### PERMANOVA

```{r}
# Permutational MANOVA (PERMANOVA)
perm_result <- vegan::adonis2(
  ratio_trait_per_pop[,c("log_ratio_L_O", "log_ratio_S_G", "log_ratio_plant_heigth")] ~ Type, 
  data = ratio_trait_per_pop, 
  method = "euclidean",
  permutations = 999
)

print(perm_result)
```

##### Pairwise PERMANOVA

```{r}
library(pairwiseAdonis) # https://github.com/pmartinezarbizu/pairwiseAdonis

pairwise_result <- pairwise.adonis2(
  ratio_trait_per_pop[,c("log_ratio_L_O", "log_ratio_S_G", "log_ratio_plant_heigth")] ~ Type,
  data = ratio_trait_per_pop,
  method = "euclidean",
  p.adjust.m = "bonferroni"
)
pairwise_result
```

### Leaf Shape

Is there a difference in the observed Leaf Shape (Elongated or not Elongated (Oval)) between the type of environment, while taking into account the different effect that Trays and Population have on the dependent variable.
#### Pop
```{r}
elongated_pop_model <- glm(elongated ~ Pop, family = "quasibinomial", data = tplant)


summary(elongated_pop_model)
```
```{r}
anova(elongated_pop_model, test = "F")
```

#### Type

```{r}
elongated_type_model <- lme4::glmer(elongated ~  Type + (1|Pop) + (1|Tray),
                                family = binomial(), data = tplant)

summary(elongated_type_model)
```
```{r}
emmeans::contrast(emmeans::emmeans(elongated_type_model, ~ Type), method = "pairwise", adjust = "tukey")
```
```{r}
predictions <- emmeans::emmeans(elongated_type_model, ~ Type, type = "response") 
cld_result <- multcomp::cld(predictions, Letters = letters, adjust = "tukey")
```
```{r}
ggplot(cld_result |> as.data.frame(), aes(x = Type, y = prob)) +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = asymp.LCL, ymax = asymp.UCL), 
                width = 0.2) +
  geom_text(aes(label = trimws(.group), 
                y = asymp.UCL + 0.1), 
            size = 5) +
  labs(x = "Surface Type",
       y = "Probability of Elongation") +
  theme_minimal() +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman")
  ) 
```



### Leaf Edge

Is there a difference in the observed Leaf Edge (Serrated or Smooth) between the type of environment, while taking into account the different effect that Trays and Population have on the dependent variable.

#### Pop
```{r}
serrated_pop_model <- glm(serrated ~ Pop, family = "quasibinomial", data = tplant)


summary(serrated_pop_model)
```
```{r}
anova(serrated_pop_model, test = "F")
```

#### Type
```{r}
serrated_type_model <- lme4::glmer(serrated ~  Type + (1|Pop) + (1|Tray),
                                family = binomial(), data = tplant)

summary(serrated_type_model)
```

```{r}
emmeans::contrast(emmeans::emmeans(serrated_type_model, ~ Type), method = "pairwise", adjust = "tukey")
```

```{r}
predictions <- emmeans::emmeans(serrated_type_model, ~ Type, type = "response") 
cld_result <- multcomp::cld(predictions, Letters = letters, adjust = "tukey")

ggplot(cld_result |> as.data.frame(), aes(x = Type, y = prob)) +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = asymp.LCL, ymax = asymp.UCL), 
                width = 0.2) +
  geom_text(aes(label = trimws(.group), 
                y = asymp.UCL + 0.1), 
            size = 5) +
  labs(x = "Surface Type",
       y = "Probability of Serration") +
  theme_minimal() +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman")
  ) 
```

### Shoot Axis

```{r}
# hist(tplant$n_shoot_axis)
# left skewed so choosing negative binomial family for glmm
```

```{r}
# axis_model <- glmmTMB::glmmTMB(n_shoot_axis ~ Type + (1|Tray) + (1|Pop),
#                       data = tplant,
#                       family = glmmTMB::nbinom2())
# 
# 
# summary(axis_model)
```

### Leaf morphology

```{r}
tplant <- tplant |>
  mutate(leaf_morph = case_when(serrated == 1 & elongated == 1 ~ "Serrated & Elongated",
                                serrated == 0 & elongated == 1 ~ "Smooth & Elongated",
                                serrated == 1 & elongated == 0 ~ "Serrated & Oval",
                                serrated == 0 & elongated == 0 ~ "Smooth & Oval",
                                T ~ NA))
```

```{r}
leaf_type_table <- table(tplant$leaf_morph, tplant$Type)
print(leaf_type_table)

# Perform the chi-square test
chi_test <- chisq.test(leaf_type_table)
print(chi_test)
```

```{r}

leaf_type_table_df <- as.data.frame(leaf_type_table) |>
  rename(Type = Var2,
         Morphology = Var1)

ggplot(leaf_type_table_df, 
       aes(x = Type, y = Freq, fill = Morphology)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Serrated & Elongated" = "#6a176e", "Serrated & Oval" = "#f8cd37",
               "Smooth & Elongated" = "#bc3754", "Smooth & Oval" = "#f37819")) +
  labs(x = "Type", y = "Count of Trait Combination", fill = "Leaf Morphology:") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman")
  )



```

```{r}
corrplot::corrplot(chi_test$residuals, is.cor = FALSE)
```

Red circle means avoided by plant, blue circle means preferred.

# F6 Experiment

Load in the table containing the random subsample of the urban cologne F5 line, which will be used in the following experiment. The table contains plants used for a new F6 generation. New ID's where given to the plants.

```{r}
# Table for the plants that are sown to produce an F6 generation.
# Plants were chosen from populations with more than 3 individuals and reduced to not carry more than 10 individuals, based on the information at hand at that time. If an individual was not at hand it was replaced by an individual at hand at random, producing the table.
tplant_F6_random <- read_csv(
  file = "data/tplant_F6_random_used_2025_07_07.csv",
  na = "na",
  col_types = cols(
    ID = col_character(),
    ID_F5_parent = col_character(),
    Pop = col_character(),
    Individuum = col_character(),
    Tray = col_character(),
    Column = col_character(),
    Row = col_character(),
    FT = col_date(),
    sowing_date_then_stratification = col_date(),
    brought_to_greenhouse = col_date(),
    not_germinated_til_2025_06_02 = col_logical(),
    not_germinated_til_2025_06_04 = col_logical(),
    not_germinated_til_2025_07_04 = col_logical(),
    leaf_shape = col_character(),
    leaf_edge = col_character(),
    n_shoot_axis = col_integer(),
    height_stem = col_integer()
  )
) |>
  mutate(
    not_germinated_til_2025_06_02 = replace_na(not_germinated_til_2025_06_02, F),
    not_germinated_til_2025_06_04 = replace_na(not_germinated_til_2025_06_04, F),
    not_germinated_til_2025_07_04 = replace_na(not_germinated_til_2025_07_04, F),
    n_shoot_axis = replace_na(n_shoot_axis, 0)
  )

tplant_F6_random <- tplant_F6_random |>
  left_join(Phenology, join_by("Pop" == "Site_ID")) |>
  mutate(
    Type = case_when(
      Type == "wall" ~ "Wall",
      Type == "grass" ~ "Grass",
      Type == "pavement" ~ "Pavement",
      TRUE ~ Type
    ),
    Pop = stringi::stri_extract(ID_F5_parent, regex = "^.*(?=-)"),
    height_stem = replace_na(height_stem, 0)
  )

```

```{r}
 tplant_F6_random_germinated <- tplant_F6_random |>
  filter(not_germinated_til_2025_07_04 == F)
```

```{r}
tplant_F6_random_germinated <- tplant_F6_random_germinated |>
  mutate(
    light_till_FT = as.integer(FT - brought_to_greenhouse),
    stratification_days = as.integer(brought_to_greenhouse - sowing_date_then_stratification),
    FT_early = case_when(light_till_FT >= 1 ~ 1,
                         T ~ 0),
    light_till_FT_maxed = replace_na(light_till_FT, 100)
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(everything(), notes)
```

## Flowering Time (FT)

### Binary Early- vs. Late Flowering

```{r}
FT_model <- lme4::glmer(FT_early ~ Type + (1|Pop) + (1|Tray) + (1|stratification_days),
                               family = binomial(), data = tplant_F6_random_germinated)
# Tray and stratification as random effect did not influence the model

summary(FT_model)
```

```{r}
plot(FT_model)
```

```{r}
# overdispersion?
simulationOutput <- simulateResiduals(fittedModel = FT_model)
testDispersion(simulationOutput)
# no overdispersion
```

### Maxed FT

Not germinated plants flowering time is set to 100

#### Pop

```{r}
FT_maxed_model <- lme4::glmer(light_till_FT_maxed ~ Type + (1|Pop) + (1|Tray) + (1|stratification_days),
                               family = poisson(), data = tplant_F6_random_germinated)

summary(FT_maxed_model)
```

```{r}
anova(FT_maxed_pop_model, test = "Chisq")
```

#### Type
```{r}
FT_maxed_type_model <- lme4::glmer(light_till_FT_maxed ~ Type + (1|Pop) + (1|Tray) + (1|stratification_days),
                               family = poisson(), data = tplant_F6_random_germinated)

summary(FT_maxed_type_model)
```

```{r}
car::Anova(FT_maxed_type_model, type = "III")
```


## Load Dormancy Count Table

```{r}
# Count data of germination rate of seeds from the F5 generation
dormancy_count <- read_csv(
  file = "data/dormancy count_2025_07_09_a.csv",
  na = "na",
  col_types = cols(
    ID_F5_parent = col_character(),
    seed_count_germinated = col_integer(),
    seed_count_total = col_integer(),
    has_fungi = col_logical(),
    has_strong_fungi = col_logical(),
    dormtest_start = col_date(),
    dormtest_end = col_date(),
    group = col_factor(),
    run = col_character(),
    Notes = col_character()
  )
) |>
  mutate(
    ID_F5_parent = case_when(
      ID_F5_parent == "165-4" ~ "156-4",
      ID_F5_parent == "165-8" ~ "156-8",
      ID_F5_parent == "165-17" ~ "156-17",
      TRUE ~ ID_F5_parent
    ),
    Pop = as.factor(stringi::stri_extract(ID_F5_parent, regex = "^.*(?=-)")),
  ) |>
  left_join(Phenology, join_by("Pop" == "Site_ID")) |>
  mutate(
    Type = as.factor(case_when(
      Type == "wall" ~ "Wall",
      Type == "grass" ~ "Grass",
      Type == "pavement" ~ "Pavement",
      TRUE ~ Type)
    )
  )
```

```{r}
# find duplicates
# dormancy_count$ID_F5_parent[duplicated(dormancy_count$ID_F5_parent)]

# add new F6 ID to the table
dormancy_count <- dormancy_count |>
  left_join(tplant_F6_random |> 
              dplyr::select(ID, ID_F5_parent), join_by("ID_F5_parent"), relationship = "many-to-many") |>
  dplyr::select(ID, ID_F5_parent, Pop, everything()) |>
  mutate(Pop = as.factor(Pop),
         ID = as.factor(ID))
```


```{r}
# get germination ratio
dormancy_germ_ratio_no_fungi <- dormancy_count |>
  filter(has_strong_fungi == FALSE) |>
  mutate(
    germ_ratio = case_when(
      seed_count_germinated == 0 ~ 0,
      seed_count_germinated == seed_count_total ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ seed_count_germinated / seed_count_total
      ),
    weeks_elapsed = case_when(run == 1 ~ 0, run == 2 ~ 3, run == 3 ~ 6) |> as.factor(),
    run = as.factor(run),
    group = as.factor(group)
  )
```

## Dormancy model
mmrm = Mixed model for repeated measurements

```{r}
library(mmrm)
```

#### Pop
##### glm
```{r}
dormancy_pop_model <- glm(
  germ_ratio ~ Pop, family = gaussian(),
  data = dormancy_germ_ratio_no_fungi |> filter(run == 3)
)
summary(dormancy_pop_model)
```

```{r}
car::Anova(dormancy_pop_model, type = "III")
```


##### MMRM

```{r}
mmrm_dormancy_pop_model <- mmrm(
  germ_ratio ~ Pop + us(run | ID),
  data = dormancy_germ_ratio_no_fungi
)

summary(mmrm_dormancy_pop_model)
```

```{r}
car::Anova(mmrm_dormancy_pop_model, type = "III")
```

#### Type

##### mmrm
```{r}
mmrm_dormancy_type_model <- mmrm(
  germ_ratio ~ Type + us(run | ID),
  data = dormancy_germ_ratio_no_fungi
)

summary(mmrm_dormancy_type_model)
```

```{r}
car::Anova(mmrm_dormancy_pop_model, type = "III")
```

```{r}
time_summary <- dormancy_germ_ratio_no_fungi %>%
  group_by(run, Type) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_germ = mean(germ_ratio, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_germ = sd(germ_ratio, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    n = n()
  ) %>%
  mutate(weeks_elapsed = case_when(
    run == "1" ~ 0,
    run == "2" ~ 3,
    run == "3" ~ 6
  ))
```

```{r}
ggplot(time_summary, aes(x = weeks_elapsed, y = mean_germ, color = Type)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_germ - se_germ, ymax = mean_germ + se_germ), 
                width = 0.2) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0, 3, 6), labels = c("Run 1", "Run 2", "Run 3")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "Wall" = "#f8cd37",
    "Grass" = "#6a176e",
    "Pavement" = "#bc3754",
    "Tree bed" = "#f37819"
  )) +
  labs(
    x = "Time (weeks)",
    y = "Mean Germination Ratio",
    title = "Dormancy Loss Over Time by Environment Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

### Dormancy Model for each run

Run 1:

```{r}
dormancy_model_run_1 <- lme4::glmer(germ_ratio ~ 
                       Type + (1|Pop) + (1|group),
                       family = gaussian(), 
                       data = dormancy_germ_ratio_no_fungi |> filter(run == 1))

summary(dormancy_model_run_1)
```

Run 2:

```{r}
dormancy_model_run_2 <- lme4::glmer(germ_ratio ~ 
                       Type + (1|Pop) + (1|group),
                       family = gaussian(), 
                       data = dormancy_germ_ratio_no_fungi |> filter(run == 2))

summary(dormancy_model_run_2)
```

Run 3:

```{r}
dormancy_model_run_3 <- lme4::glmer(germ_ratio ~ 
                       Type + (1|Pop) + (1|group),
                       family = gaussian(), 
                       data = dormancy_germ_ratio_no_fungi |> filter(run == 3))

summary(dormancy_model_run_3)
```

```{r}
emmeans::contrast(emmeans::emmeans(dormancy_model_run_3, ~ Type), method = "pairwise", adjust = "tukey")
```

```{r}
# overdispersion?
simulationOutput2 <- simulateResiduals(fittedModel = dormancy_model_run_3)
testDispersion(simulationOutput2)
# no overdispersion
```

## Leaf Morphology of F6

### Leaf Edge

```{r}
# simplify leaf morphology:
# WS = weak serrated
# H = half the leaf is serrated, from where the petiole meets the blade to the 
# middle of the blade
# S = serrated
# 
# all will be considered serrated

# G is not serrated but smooth leaf margin

tplant_F6_random_germinated <- tplant_F6_random_germinated |>
  mutate(leaf_edge_simple = case_when(leaf_edge %in% c("WS", "H") ~ "S",
                                      T ~ leaf_edge),
         serrated = case_when(leaf_edge_simple == "S" ~ 1,
                               leaf_edge_simple == "G" ~ 0,
                               T ~ NA))
```

#### Pop
```{r}
serrated_pop2_model <- glm(serrated ~ Pop, family = "quasibinomial", data = tplant_F6_random_germinated)


summary(serrated_pop2_model)
```

```{r}
anova(serrated_pop2_model, test = "F")
```

#### Type
```{r}
serrated_type2_model <- lme4::glmer(
  serrated ~ Type + (1|Tray) + (1|Pop),
  family = binomial(),
  data = tplant_F6_random_germinated
)
summary(serrated_type2_model)
```

```{r}
emmeans::contrast(emmeans::emmeans(serrated_type2_model, ~ Type), method = "pairwise", adjust = "tukey")
```
```{r}
predictions <- emmeans::emmeans(serrated_type2_model, ~ Type, type = "response") 
cld_result <- multcomp::cld(predictions, Letters = letters, adjust = "tukey")

ggplot(cld_result |> as.data.frame(), aes(x = Type, y = prob)) +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = asymp.LCL, ymax = asymp.UCL), 
                width = 0.2) +
  geom_text(aes(label = trimws(.group), 
                y = asymp.UCL - 0.1), 
            size = 5) +
  labs(x = "Surface Type",
       y = "Probability of Serration") +
  theme_minimal() +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman")
  ) 
```


### Leaf Shape

```{r}
# simplify leaf morphology:
# OL = Oval but also elongated
# O+L = Rosette contains Oval and elongated leaves
#
# they will be excluded
#
# L = elongated
# O = oval or round leaf shape

tplant_F6_random_germinated <- tplant_F6_random_germinated |>
  mutate(elongated = case_when(leaf_shape == "L" ~ 1,
                               leaf_shape == "O" ~ 0,
                               T ~ NA))
```

#### Pop
```{r}
elongated_pop2_model <- glm(elongated ~ Pop, family = "quasibinomial", data = tplant_F6_random_germinated)


summary(elongated_pop2_model)
```

```{r}
anova(elongated_pop2_model, test = "F") # it is genetically determined
```

#### Type
```{r}
elongated_type2_model <- lme4::glmer(elongated ~ Type + (1|Tray) + (1|Pop),
                                     family = binomial(),
                                     data = tplant_F6_random_germinated)

summary(elongated_type2_model)
```

```{r}
emmeans::contrast(
  emmeans::emmeans(elongated_type2_model, ~ Type),
  method = "pairwise",
  adjust = "tukey"
)
```

```{r}
predictions <- emmeans::emmeans(elongated_type2_model, ~ Type, type = "response") 
cld_result <- multcomp::cld(predictions, Letters = letters, adjust = "tukey")

ggplot(cld_result |> as.data.frame(), aes(x = Type, y = prob)) +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = asymp.LCL, ymax = asymp.UCL), 
                width = 0.2) +
  geom_text(aes(label = trimws(.group), 
                y = asymp.UCL + 0.1), 
            size = 5) +
  labs(x = "Surface Type",
       y = "Probability of Elongation") +
  theme_minimal() +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman")
  ) 
```


### Shoot Axis

```{r}
# hist(tplant_F6_random_germinated$n_shoot_axis)
# left skewed so choosing negative binomial family for glmm
```

```{r}
# axis2_model <- glmmTMB::glmmTMB(n_shoot_axis ~ Type + (1|Tray)+ (1|Pop),
#                       data = tplant_F6_random_germinated,
#                       family = glmmTMB::nbinom2())
# 
# 
# summary(axis2_model)
```

### Leaf morphology

#### smooth x serrated x elongated x oval

```{r}
tplant_F6_random_germinated <- tplant_F6_random_germinated |>
  mutate(leaf_morph = case_when(serrated == 1 & elongated == 1 ~ "Serrated & Elongated",
                                serrated == 0 & elongated == 1 ~ "Smooth & Elongated",
                                serrated == 1 & elongated == 0 ~ "Serrated & Oval",
                                serrated == 0 & elongated == 0 ~ "Smooth & Oval",
                                T ~ NA))
```

```{r}
leaf_type_table2 <- table(tplant_F6_random_germinated$leaf_morph,
                          tplant_F6_random_germinated$Type)
print(leaf_type_table2)

# Perform the chi-square test
chi_test2 <- chisq.test(leaf_type_table2)
print(chi_test2)
# not significant, not enough plants with smooth leaf margin
```

not. significant, and not enough values (multiple below 5).

#### !(smooth) x serrated x elongated x oval

Model without problematic smooth leaf margin type, which is underrepresented:

```{r}
tplant_F6_random_germinated <- tplant_F6_random_germinated |>
  mutate(
    leaf_morph_no_smooth = case_when(
      serrated == 1 & elongated == 1 ~ "Serrated & Elongated",
      serrated == 1 &
        elongated == 0 ~ "Serrated & Oval",
      T ~ NA
    )
  )
```

```{r}
leaf_type_table3 <- table(tplant_F6_random_germinated$leaf_morph_no_smooth,
                          tplant_F6_random_germinated$Type)
print(leaf_type_table3)

# Perform the chi-square test
chi_test2 <- chisq.test(leaf_type_table3)
print(chi_test2)
```

not. sign.

```{r}
leaf_edge_shape_proportions <- tplant_F6_random_germinated |> 
  mutate(serrated_plot = case_when(serrated == 1 ~ "S",
                                   serrated == 0 ~ "G"),
         elongated_plot = case_when(elongated == 1 ~ "L",
                                    elongated == 0 ~ "O")) |>  # Fixed: changed + to |>
  mutate(Leaf_edge_shape = paste0(elongated_plot, serrated_plot)) |>
  count(Type, Leaf_edge_shape, name = "count") |>  # Fixed: changed leaf_morph to Leaf_edge_shape
  group_by(Type) |> 
  mutate(proportion = round(count / sum(count), 2)) |> 
  select(-count) |> 
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = Leaf_edge_shape, 
    values_from = proportion,
    names_prefix = "prop_",  
    values_fill = 0
  ) |> 
  ungroup()

leaf_edge_shape_long <- leaf_edge_shape_proportions |> 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(prop_LG, prop_LS, prop_OG, prop_OS),
    names_to = "Leaf_edge_shape",
    values_to = "Proportion"
  )

ggplot(leaf_edge_shape_long, 
       aes(x = Type, y = Proportion, fill = Leaf_edge_shape)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  scale_fill_manual(
  values = c("prop_LG" = "#440154", "prop_LS" = "#31688e",
             "prop_OG" = "#35b779", "prop_OS" = "#fde725"),
  labels = c("Long Smooth", "Long Serrated", "Oval Smooth", "Oval Serrated")
) +
  labs(x = "Type", y = "Proportion of Leaf Morphology", fill = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 15, family = "Times New Roman")
  )



```

### MANOVA

Is there a Population-level difference between the observed traits between the types of Environment?

```{r}
# compute a ratio of leaf shape and leaf edge for each population from the individuals in each population
ratio_trait_per_pop2 <- tplant_F6_random_germinated |>
  drop_na(elongated, serrated) |> # remove plants, which could not be categorized
  group_by(Pop, Type, height_stem) |> # group by Population
  summarise(
    # compute the log ratio of elongated leaf shape over oval leaf shape
    n_L = sum(elongated == 1), # sum up the count of elongated leaf per population
    n_O = sum(elongated == 0), # sum up the count of oval leaf per population
    # avoid division by 0 by adding 0.01 (laplace smoothing)
    log_ratio_L_O = log((n_L + 0.01) / (n_O + 0.01)),
    # zero means same proportion, positive number means more elongated leaf shape (L), negative oval leaf shape (O)
    
    # compute the log ratio of serrated leaf edge over not serrated leaf edge
    n_S = sum(serrated == 1),# sum up the count of serrated leaf per population
    n_G = sum(serrated == 0),# sum up the count of not serrated leaf per population
    log_ratio_S_G = log((n_S + 0.01) / (n_G + 0.01)),
    ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(Type = as.factor(Type)) 
```

#### MANOVA Assumptions

```{r}
table(ratio_trait_per_pop2$Type)

colnames(ratio_trait_per_pop2)

boxplot(log_ratio_L_O ~ Type, data = ratio_trait_per_pop2) # outlier
boxplot(log_ratio_S_G ~ Type, data = ratio_trait_per_pop2) # outlier
boxplot(height_stem ~ Type, data = ratio_trait_per_pop2) # no outlier

library(mvnormtest)

by(ratio_trait_per_pop2[,c("log_ratio_L_O","log_ratio_S_G", "height_stem")], ratio_trait_per_pop2$Type, 
   function(x) mshapiro.test(t(x)))
# violation of normality 
```

MANOVA assumptions not met (normality violation). Using PERMANOVA instead.

#### PERMANOVA

```{r}
# Permutational MANOVA (PERMANOVA)
perm_result <- vegan::adonis2(
  ratio_trait_per_pop2[,c("log_ratio_L_O","log_ratio_S_G", "height_stem")] ~ Type, 
  data = ratio_trait_per_pop2, 
  method = "euclidean",
  permutations = 999
)

perm_result
```

not sign. different.

### Leaf morphology
```{r}
tplant_F6_random_germinated <- tplant_F6_random_germinated |>
  mutate(leaf_morph = case_when(serrated == 1 & elongated == 1 ~ "Serrated & Elongated",
                                serrated == 0 & elongated == 1 ~ "Smooth & Elongated",
                                serrated == 1 & elongated == 0 ~ "Serrated & Oval",
                                serrated == 0 & elongated == 0 ~ "Smooth & Oval",
                                T ~ NA))
```

```{r}
leaf_type_table2 <- table(tplant_F6_random_germinated$leaf_morph, tplant_F6_random_germinated$Type)
print(leaf_type_table2)
```

```{r}
leaf_type_table2_df <- as.data.frame(leaf_type_table2) |>
  rename(Type = Var2,
         Morphology = Var1)

ggplot(leaf_type_table2_df, 
       aes(x = Type, y = Freq, fill = Morphology)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Serrated & Elongated" = "#6a176e", "Serrated & Oval" = "#f8cd37",
               "Smooth & Elongated" = "#bc3754", "Smooth & Oval" = "#f37819")) +
  labs(x = "Type", y = "Count of Trait Combination", fill = "Leaf Morphology:") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman")
  )
```

### Correlation
```{r}
tplant_F6_random_germinated_renamed <- tplant_F6_random_germinated |>
  rename_with(~ paste0(.x, "_F6"), -c(ID, ID_F5_parent))

tplant_renamed <- tplant |>
  rename_with(~ paste0(.x, "_F5"), -c(ID))

F5_and_F6_joined <- tplant_F6_random_germinated_renamed |>
  left_join(
    tplant_renamed,
    by = c("ID_F5_parent" = "ID")
  ) |>
  rename(Type = Type_F6,
         Pop = Pop_F6)
```

```{r}
ratio_trait_per_pop_F6 <- tplant_F6_random_germinated |>
  drop_na(elongated, serrated) |> # remove plants, which could not be categorized
  group_by(Pop, Type) |> # group by Population
  summarise(
    # compute the log ratio of elongated leaf shape over oval leaf shape
    n_L = sum(elongated == T), # sum up the count of elongated leaf per population
    n_O = sum(elongated == F), # sum up the count of oval leaf per population
    # avoid division by 0 by adding 0.01 (laplace smoothing)
    log_ratio_L_O = log((n_L + 0.01) / (n_O + 0.01)),
    # zero means same proportion, positive number means more elongated leaf shape (L), negative oval leaf shape (O)
    
    # compute the log ratio of serrated leaf edge over not serrated leaf edge
    n_S = sum(serrated == T),# sum up the count of serrated leaf per population
    n_G = sum(serrated == F),# sum up the count of not serrated leaf per population
    log_ratio_S_G = log((n_S + 0.01) / (n_G + 0.01)),
  ) |>
  ungroup()
```

```{r}
pop_ratio_joined <- ratio_trait_per_pop_F6 |>
  left_join(ratio_trait_per_pop, by = "Pop", suffix = c("_F6", "_F5"))
```

```{r}
# # find duplicates in col
# tplant %>%
#   group_by(ID) %>%
#   filter(n() > 1) %>%
#   ungroup() |>
#   pull(ID)
```

### Leaf shape
```{r}
corr_elon <- glm(log_ratio_L_O_F5 ~ log_ratio_L_O_F6, data = pop_ratio_joined,
                 family = gaussian())

summary(corr_elon)
```

```{r}
ggplot(data = pop_ratio_joined, aes(x = log_ratio_L_O_F5, y = log_ratio_L_O_F6)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Type_F6)) + 
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = F)
```

### Leaf edge
```{r}
corr_edge <- glm(log_ratio_S_G_F5 ~ log_ratio_S_G_F6, data = pop_ratio_joined,
                 family = gaussian())

summary(corr_edge)
```

```{r}
ggplot(data = pop_ratio_joined, aes(x = log_ratio_S_G_F5, y = log_ratio_S_G_F6, color = )) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Type_F6)) + 
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = F)
```
